proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 35 continuous patient features, at least one is strongly correlated (Spearman |rho| > 0.5) with pfs_months.
feature_013 (range 0.1-3622, highly right-skewed) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_013 -> shorter pfs_months.
feature_078 (range 30-90, median 65) is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.spearmanr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman correlation between feature_078 and pfs_months is rho=+0.853 (p<1e-300); the linear slope is +0.174 months of PFS per unit increase in feature_078. This is the dominant univariate signal in the dataset.

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr(df['feature_013'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 vs pfs_months Spearman rho=-0.223 (p<1e-300); raw linear slope -0.0033 months/unit. Direction confirms hypothesis: higher feature_013 -> shorter PFS.

analyses.code: for c in 35 continuous cols: stats.spearmanr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Of 35 continuous features scanned, 4 had |Spearman rho| > 0.10: feature_078 (+0.853), feature_013 (-0.223), feature_006 (-0.134), feature_009 (+0.118). Only feature_078 exceeds the |rho|>0.5 threshold.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (range 0-24.6) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_006 -> shorter PFS.
feature_009 (range 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_009 -> longer PFS.

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr(df['feature_006'], df['pfs_months']); stats.linregress(df['feature_006'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 Spearman rho=-0.134 (p=4e-200); linear slope -0.0749 months PFS per unit (p=1e-225). Direction matches hypothesis.

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr(df['feature_009'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_009 Spearman rho=+0.118 (p=1e-154); linear slope +0.495 months PFS per unit (p=1e-166). Direction matches hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, feature_051 (prevalence ~55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months when present (=1) than when absent (=0).
Most binary features are NOT individually associated with pfs_months (after correction for multiple testing across the 77 binary features).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_051']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_051']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months is 3.51 when feature_051=1 vs 4.03 when feature_051=0 (Welch t=-28.85, p=2e-181). Difference -0.52 months; direction matches hypothesis.

analyses.code: for c in 77 binary cols: stats.ttest_ind(...)
analyses.result_summary: Of 77 binary features tested, only 1 (feature_051) survives Bonferroni correction (p<0.05/77 = 6.5e-4). Five features reach nominal p<0.05 (feature_051, feature_109, feature_039, feature_105, feature_043), consistent with hypothesis that most binary features have no marginal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five levels of feature_018 (race-like categorical with values asian/black/hispanic/white/other).
Mean pfs_months differs across the four levels of feature_045 (insurance-like categorical with values medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured).
Black vs white patients (within feature_018) have shorter mean pfs_months.
Medicaid or uninsured patients (within feature_045) have shorter mean pfs_months than privately insured patients.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_018']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df['feature_018'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA across feature_018 levels: F=0.564, p=0.689. Group means range from 3.737 (hispanic) to 3.797 (asian); no detectable heterogeneity in mean PFS by race.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(...)
analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA across feature_045 levels: F=0.344, p=0.794. Group means cluster between 3.726 (uninsured) and 3.754 (medicare); no detectable heterogeneity by insurance.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_018']=='black','pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_018']=='white','pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: black 3.741 vs white 3.741, difference -0.0004 months (t=-0.02, p=0.987). No black-vs-white disparity in PFS detected; hypothesis refuted.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of medicaid vs private and uninsured vs private
analyses.result_summary: Medicaid (3.741) vs private (3.736): diff +0.005, p=0.871. Uninsured (3.726) vs private (3.736): diff -0.010, p=0.831. No insurance-related disparity in PFS detected; hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment for the top continuous predictors (feature_078, log feature_013, feature_006, feature_009) and the strongest binary predictors (feature_051, feature_109, feature_039), feature_078 remains the most influential predictor of pfs_months.
After full multivariable adjustment, the univariate feature_051 effect (-0.52 months) is substantially attenuated, indicating that feature_051 is confounded by the top continuous predictors.
After full multivariable adjustment, race (feature_018) and insurance (feature_045) remain non-significant predictors of pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + log_feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_105 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.822. feature_078 dominates with t=452.9 (coef +0.174 per unit, p<<1e-300). log_feature_013 t=-101.5, feature_006 t=-77.8, feature_009 t=+62.5. All four are highly significant. Hypothesis supported.

analyses.code: Same multivariable model; inspect feature_051 coefficient
analyses.result_summary: In the multivariable model, the feature_051 coefficient drops to +0.0009 (p=0.92), down from a univariate effect of -0.52 months. The univariate feature_051 effect is fully explained by joint distribution with feature_078, feature_013, feature_006, feature_009.

analyses.code: Coefficient block for race / insurance dummies in the multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: All race indicator p-values >= 0.37 (largest |coef| 0.018 for 'other' vs white). All insurance indicator p-values >= 0.29 (largest |coef| 0.0089 for medicare vs private). After adjustment, neither race nor insurance is associated with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Including ALL 122 features simultaneously in an OLS model identifies a small, parsimonious set (<= 10) of independently significant predictors of pfs_months.
feature_092 (range 48-830, median 211) is independently associated with pfs_months after adjusting for the dominant continuous predictors.
feature_109 (10% prevalent binary) and feature_039 (10% prevalent binary) are independently associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(y, sm.add_constant(pd.concat([all_continuous, all_binary, race_dummies, insurance_dummies], axis=1))).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full 122-feature OLS achieves R^2=0.8226 (essentially identical to the 4-predictor core model R^2=0.822). Only 9 features pass Bonferroni at p<4e-4 across ~123 tests: feature_078 (+0.174, p~0), log_feature_013 (-0.19, p~0), feature_006 (-0.077, p~0), feature_009 (+0.477, p~0), feature_092 (-5.5e-4, p=7e-31), feature_109 (+0.137, p=7e-27), feature_039 (+0.130, p=6e-25), feature_027 (+0.036, p=5e-6), feature_043 (-0.042, p=1e-5). Hypothesis supported.

analyses.code: Coefficient on feature_092 in full multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: feature_092 has independent effect coef=-0.000547 per unit (t=-11.6, p=7e-31). At median feature_092=211 and SD~150, a one-SD change implies about -0.08 months PFS. Direction: higher feature_092 -> shorter PFS.

analyses.code: Coefficients on feature_109 and feature_039 in full multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: feature_109=1 vs 0 adds +0.137 months PFS (p=7e-27). feature_039=1 vs 0 adds +0.130 months PFS (p=6e-25). Both protective and statistically robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Top continuous predictors (feature_078, log feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_092) and the binary predictors feature_109, feature_039, feature_051 have similar means / prevalences across feature_018 (race) groups.
Top continuous and binary predictors have similar means / prevalences across feature_045 (insurance) groups.

analyses.code: ANOVA / chi-square of each top predictor vs feature_018
analyses.result_summary: Across 5 race groups: feature_078 ANOVA p=0.674; log_feature_013 p=0.690; feature_006 p=0.317; feature_009 p=0.465; feature_092 p=0.669; feature_109 chi^2 p=0.434; feature_039 chi^2 p=0.261; feature_051 chi^2 p=0.818. None reach p<0.05; predictors are well balanced across race. Hypothesis supported.

analyses.code: ANOVA / chi-square of each top predictor vs feature_045
analyses.result_summary: Across 4 insurance groups: feature_078 p=0.881; log_feature_013 p=0.182; feature_006 p=0.420; feature_009 p=0.216; feature_092 p=0.785; feature_109 p=0.400; feature_039 p=0.603; feature_051 p=0.372. All p>=0.18. Predictors are well balanced across insurance. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 differs across feature_018 (race) groups: i.e., some racial groups gain more PFS per unit feature_078 than others.
The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 differs across feature_045 (insurance) groups.
After conditioning on the multivariable model, the residual pfs_months differs systematically by feature_018 or feature_045 (e.g., a group is consistently under- or over-predicted).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <base> + feature_078:C(feature_018, Treatment(reference="white"))', data=df).fit().f_test(...)
analyses.result_summary: Joint F-test of the four feature_078 x race interaction terms: F=0.389, p=0.817. Race-stratified Spearman rho between feature_078 and PFS is essentially identical: white 0.853, black 0.851, hispanic 0.858, asian 0.847, other 0.849. Race-stratified linear slopes 0.171-0.175. No race heterogeneity in feature_078 effect.

analyses.code: Joint F-test of feature_078 x insurance interactions
analyses.result_summary: Joint F=0.301, p=0.825. The three insurance interaction coefficients are all <= 0.0016/unit in absolute value with p>=0.45. No insurance heterogeneity in feature_078 effect.

analyses.code: Fit base model w/o demographics, ANOVA of residuals across feature_018 / feature_045
analyses.result_summary: Mean residual by race: white +0.002, black -0.0004, hispanic -0.008, asian +0.005, other -0.019. ANOVA F=0.337, p=0.853. Mean residual by insurance: medicare +0.004, private -0.005, medicaid -0.0001, uninsured +0.005. ANOVA F=0.433, p=0.730. No systematic prediction error by demographic group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: log feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_109, and feature_039 each have effects that vary across feature_018 (race) levels (i.e., a racial subgroup is differentially sensitive).
The same five predictors have effects that vary across feature_045 (insurance) levels.

analyses.code: Joint F-tests of predictor x C(feature_018) for each of {log_feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_109, feature_039}
analyses.result_summary: All five joint F-tests fail to reject: log_feature_013 F=0.17 p=0.96; feature_006 F=0.91 p=0.46; feature_009 F=1.41 p=0.23; feature_109 F=0.93 p=0.45; feature_039 F=0.15 p=0.96. No race heterogeneity for any predictor.

analyses.code: Joint F-tests of predictor x C(feature_045)
analyses.result_summary: All five joint F-tests fail to reject: log_feature_013 F=0.48 p=0.70; feature_006 F=0.59 p=0.62; feature_009 F=0.47 p=0.70; feature_109 F=0.62 p=0.60; feature_039 F=1.11 p=0.34. No insurance heterogeneity for any predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a significant feature_078 x log feature_013 interaction: at higher feature_078, the negative slope of pfs_months on log feature_013 is steeper (more negative).
There is a significant feature_078 x feature_009 interaction: at higher feature_078, the positive feature_009 slope is amplified.
There is a significant feature_078 x feature_006 interaction.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <core> + feature_078:log_feature_013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_078:log_feature_013 = -0.00354 (p=9e-30). Implied effect: at feature_078=30, log_feature_013 slope = -0.19 + 30*(-0.00354) = -0.30; at feature_078=90, slope = -0.51. Confirms the high-feature_078 amplification of negative log_feature_013 effect.

analyses.code: Add feature_078:feature_009 to base model
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.00403 (p=1.4e-7). At feature_078=30, feature_009 slope ~ 0.232 + 30*0.00403 = 0.353; at feature_078=90 ~ 0.595. Confirms amplification of feature_009 effect at higher feature_078.

analyses.code: Add feature_078:feature_006 to base model
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.000651 (p=5.7e-11). Negative direction: at higher feature_078 the negative feature_006 effect is amplified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months has positive curvature (concave-up): the slope steepens at higher feature_078 values.
The relationship between feature_092 and pfs_months has negative curvature (concave-down/inverse-U): an intermediate feature_092 maximizes pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + I(feature_078**2) + ...', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_078^2 coef +0.000783 (p=4e-172). Together with linear coef +0.071, slope at feature_078=30: 0.071 + 2*30*0.000783 = 0.118; at feature_078=90: 0.071 + 2*90*0.000783 = 0.212. Confirms concave-up shape.

analyses.code: Add I(feature_092**2) to base model
analyses.result_summary: feature_092^2 coef -2.42e-6 (p=1e-11), with linear feature_092 coef +0.000814 (p=4e-5). Vertex at feature_092 = -0.000814/(2*-2.42e-6) ~ 168 (close to median 211). Confirms inverse-U shape.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients in the lowest quintile of feature_078 (<=56.5) have mean pfs_months below 2 months.
Patients in the highest quintile of feature_078 (>73.5) have mean pfs_months above 6 months.
The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 is approximately the same across tertiles of log feature_013.

analyses.code: df.groupby(pd.qcut(df['feature_078'], 5))['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Q1 of feature_078 (30.0-56.5, n=10006): mean PFS 1.345 (SD 1.097). Confirms hypothesis.

analyses.code: Same quintile aggregation
analyses.result_summary: Q5 of feature_078 (73.5-90.0, n=9855): mean PFS 6.240 (SD 1.354). Confirms hypothesis. The Q5-Q1 difference is 4.90 months, consistent with the linear slope of ~0.174 per unit over a 35-unit Q5-Q1 gap.

analyses.code: stats.linregress(sub['feature_078'], sub['pfs_months']) within each tertile of log_feature_013
analyses.result_summary: Slope of pfs_months on feature_078 in tertiles of log_feature_013: low 0.1775, mid 0.1757, high 0.1691. Slopes within ~5% of one another, consistent with hypothesis. Note: at the FULLY adjusted (joint) level there is a small but highly significant feature_078 x log_feature_013 interaction (h10_1); the tertile-stratified linear slopes still cluster tightly because they marginalize over correlations with other variables.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, at least one has an interaction with feature_078 that survives Bonferroni correction (p < 0.05/77 ~ 6.5e-4).
feature_051 modifies the slope of pfs_months on feature_078: when feature_051=1 the per-unit feature_078 effect is smaller (less positive) than when feature_051=0.
After multivariable adjustment in the parsimonious final model, the feature_051 main effect remains non-significant.

analyses.code: for c in 77 binary cols: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + c + feature_078:c').fit()
analyses.result_summary: Of 77 binary x feature_078 interaction tests, only 1 (feature_051) has p < 0.05/77: coef -0.00469, p=3.3e-7. Three others (feature_108, feature_052, feature_093) reach nominal p<0.05 but fail Bonferroni.

analyses.code: Coefficient on feature_078:feature_051 in pairwise model
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.00469 per unit (p=3.3e-7). At feature_078=30, the per-unit effect is 0.174 - 0.00469 ~ 0.17 if feature_051=1. The interaction is small (~3% of the main slope) but statistically robust.

analyses.code: Final parsimonious OLS with feature_051 omitted (because not independently significant)
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 main effect was already shown non-significant after adjustment in iter05 (p=0.92, coef +0.0009). It is therefore excluded from the parsimonious final model. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious final model containing feature_078, feature_078^2, log feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_092, feature_092^2, feature_109, feature_039, feature_027, feature_043 and the three feature_078 interactions explains > 82% of the variance in pfs_months and recovers essentially all the systematic signal in the dataset.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + I(feature_078**2) + log_feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_092 + I(feature_092**2) + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_027 + feature_043 + feature_078:log_feature_013 + feature_078:feature_006 + feature_078:feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final model R^2=0.8258, Adj R^2=0.8258 (vs full 122-feature OLS R^2=0.8226). All 14 terms are individually significant at p<1e-4 (smallest |t|=4.02 for feature_092 main effect). Only 0.4 percentage points of additional R^2 are obtainable from the remaining 100+ features in aggregate. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all main and interaction analyses, race (feature_018) shows no association with pfs_months and no modification of any other predictor's effect on pfs_months.
Across all main and interaction analyses, insurance (feature_045) shows no association with pfs_months and no modification of any other predictor's effect on pfs_months.
Predictor distributions are also balanced across feature_018 and feature_045, so the demographic null result reflects genuine absence of demographic effects (not residual confounding).

analyses.code: Synthesis of iters 4, 6 (race/insurance dummies in full model), 7 (covariate balance), 8-9 (demographic interactions), 8 (residual-mean ANOVA)
analyses.result_summary: Synthesis: (a) Marginal ANOVA of pfs_months by race p=0.689, by insurance p=0.794. (b) Adjusted race coefficients all p>=0.37; adjusted insurance coefficients all p>=0.29. (c) All 8 top predictors balanced across both demographics (all ANOVA/chi-square p>=0.18). (d) None of the 5 predictor x race or 5 predictor x insurance joint F-tests reach p<0.22. (e) Residual ANOVA across race p=0.853 and across insurance p=0.730. The dataset shows no detectable differential effect of race or insurance on pfs_months at any level (main effect, interaction, residual error, or covariate distribution).
